column1: Hall of Fame game canceled. We're on LIVE at 11 on ABC Action News with the latest on the tropics.

I’m really confused. Every year we take the media tracking events one step further. This thing isn’t a hurricane… or a tropical storm. It’s a mere disturbance, and all this hype. I suppose this is the age of social media, but what’s next, we start tracking 2 point drops in pressure? The world has kinda sorta lost its mind.

unless you were displaced by the flooding that Idalia brought (we lost everything in our home), and live in a flood-prone area- then it’s understandable that you may not comprehend why some of us may want a huge heads-up to try and get our property ready and evacuation plans made should they be necessary.
This was still “no big deal” yesterday, but with today’s defined track, it is- to thousands who live in our flood-prone neighborhood. Hope this answers your question.
Denis was saying the same thing last night. He has always tracked these storms like this but people are literally acting like it’s a Cat5. Maybe it’s the new people being initiated! 😂
I wear a seat belt all the time when driving knowing (hopefully) I'll never crash. But I'm always ready. More knowledge about nothing is better than no knowledge about something.
The water temps in the gulf are approaching 90 degrees. That is not suppose to happen until September. Got it now?
pretty sure I got it before. #socialMedia
Rapid intensification of tropical systems, that is what happens during an era of abrupt climate change. climate.nasa.gov

I think this has been tracked because it went from a 30% chance of development to now an 80%. There is a chance of it turning into a hurricane before it hits the panhandle area if it continues on the path they’re thinking.
